title: OpenChronology Calendar Definition Schema
description: Schema for .chroncal files — standalone, independently hostable calendar system definitions. A .chroncal file describes a single calendar: its epoch, structure (linear or cyclic), and value format. It is referenceable by any number of .chron files.

meta.description: File-level metadata for the calendar definition.

id.description: Stable machine-readable identifier (e.g. 'shire-reckoning', 'galactic-standard'). Used as the key in .chron definitions.calendars.

version.description: Semver string for this calendar definition (e.g. '1.0.0').

display_name.description: Human-readable name of the calendar (e.g. 'Shire Reckoning').

canonical_url.description: The authoritative hosted URL for this .chroncal file. SHOULD be populated for any calendar intended for external reference.

description.description: Human-readable description of the calendar system and its origin.

$schema.description: JSON Schema URL for validation tooling. Should point to this schema.

epoch.description: Anchors this calendar's year zero to a reference universe. Required for calendars that need cross-universe temporal comparison.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of the epoch (e.g. 'Year 1 SR = Third Age 1601').

reference_universe.description: Universe ID (typically 'real') that the reference_value is expressed in.

reference_value.description: ISO 8601 datetime in the reference universe corresponding to this calendar's epoch.

structure.description: Defines whether the calendar is linear (monotonic) or cyclic (month/year based). Exactly one of the structure type branches applies.

value_format.description: The format string used to parse and produce temporal.*_value strings for this calendar. Common values: 'YYYY-MM-DD', 'YYYY-MMM-DD', 'decimal', 'integer'.

linear_structure.description: For monotonic, non-cyclical scales: stardates, project day counters, geologic time.

unit_name.description: Name of a single unit in this calendar (e.g. 'stardate', 'day', 'Ma').

unit_length_seconds.description: Length of one unit in SI seconds. Required for cross-universe temporal comparison.

cyclic_structure.description: For calendars with named months, seasons, or ages. Supports intercalary days and leap rules.

year_length_days.description: Nominal number of days in a standard (non-leap) year.

months.description: Ordered array of months. Month names here are the valid values for temporal.*_value strings and recurrence.on.month fields.

name.description: Month name. Must be unique within this calendar.

days.description: Number of days in this month.

intercalary.description: True for intercalary (interstitial) periods that fall between named months.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of the leap rule.

frequency_years.description: How many years between leap years.

extra_days.description: Extra days inserted during leap years.

after_month.description: Name of the month after which the extra days are inserted.
